global_context.scene_description: A photographic interior scene of a Phoenix-style living room with a couple in their late 30s sitting together on a cream-colored couch — both visibly relaxed, both with the body language of 'thank god this is over.' The husband (white male, mid-30s, casual t-shirt, sweatpants, slight beard) sits with his arm around his wife. He has a beer in one hand, head tilted back slightly with a small relieved laugh. The wife (white woman, mid-30s, casual sweater, jeans, brown hair, no makeup needed) leans against him, holding a coffee mug, smiling — eyes slightly closed, like she's just exhaled for the first time in months. Both look genuine, NOT posed-model perfect. The room is cool, comfortable, in a warm Phoenix Southwestern style — terracotta accents, woven throw, indoor cactus. Through a large window behind them, the Phoenix afternoon outside is visibly blasting — bright bleached sun, heat haze on the horizon, dry desert. A modern Nest-style thermostat on the wall reads 72°. The contrast is the visual story: HOT outside, COOL inside, COUPLE finally relaxed. Caption text appears at the very bottom of the frame in white letters on a slim solid black bar (only the bottom 8% of frame): 'TWO YEARS OF DREAD. ONE PHONE CALL.' The whole image feels like a real moment, not a stock photo — slightly imperfect framing, casual posture, Phoenix-real.
